scan_metadata.note: Updated scan with 6 additional targeted queries beyond original 9

rejected_options.rejection_reason: Svelte 5 repeatedly encounters compilation and runtime issues in Obsidian Electron ($state/$derived compilation failures). No official Obsidian support for Svelte 5. Community adaptation has many pitfalls. M0 frontend reconstruction completely removed Svelte 5. Decision explicitly rejects continuing with Svelte 5.

Svelte 4 EOL approaching. Decision was to only conditionally introduce Svelte 4 for complex UI needs, not as primary framework. Svelte ultimately replaced by React as implementation method (fact 9efd08d9). New architecture uses React + ReactFlow + shadcn/ui.

rejected_options.option_name: React as Obsidian plugin framework (original 2026-03-12 decision)
rejected_options.rejection_reason: Originally rejected on 2026-03-12 when choosing Obsidian plugin framework because Svelte was deemed more suitable. However, this decision was REVERSED on 2026-03-17: Svelte replaced by React due to Svelte 5 Electron issues. React is now the confirmed stack.
rejected_options.note: REVERSED - React is now the chosen framework. Original rejection no longer applies.

rejected_options.rejection_reason: Rejected on 2026-03-12 during framework selection. No further evidence of reconsideration.
Rejected in Session D Pencil design rebuild. Static panels do not match the interactive learning paradigm required.
V1 had fundamentally flawed interaction paradigm. Was rebuilt into V2. Decision also rejected continuing to use browser HTML prototype for interaction validation.

rejected_options.option_name: Running demo in standalone browser page
rejected_options.rejection_reason: Decision rejected running demo as standalone browser page. Must run within Obsidian plugin environment.
New plugin is NOT based on Obsidian native Canvas extension. Will not use .canvas files or Canvas node/edge system. Product remains within Obsidian plugin framework but with completely custom UI.
New frontend will not use CRDT. Decision was explicit.

User explicitly stated it's not a rigid need. Completely disconnected from actual usage scenarios. Recommended to downgrade to non-essential. Also rejected due to code absence, lack of academic precedent, and fatal data sparsity.
User rejected 'implement first, verify later' approach (Plan B). Also rejected in later context because Plan B (backend DB) requires backend running, which contradicts offline-first needs.
Obsidian components replaced by shadcn/ui in the new architecture (2026-03-17). 6-agent research recommended shadcn/ui.
Appeared in early architecture exploration but was dropped from the confirmed new architecture. New architecture uses React + Vite + ReactFlow + shadcn/ui within Obsidian plugin.
rejected_options.note: Was briefly considered but not part of confirmed stack.

rejected_options.rejection_reason: Rejected in S8-Brainstorming session.
User decision explicitly rejects the pure Socratic auto-guidance scheme. Replaced by user-choice-based approach.
Directly giving answers decreases learning effect size (d=1.50). Rejected as part of exam whiteboard design. Constraint added: prohibit direct answers.
No successful Canvas plugin has rebuilt a whiteboard from scratch. Decision explicitly states whiteboard should not be self-built.
Rejected by PRD-Review as an option.

do_not_research: Svelte 5 in Obsidian Electron - confirmed broken, no official support, already removed
Svelte 4 as primary framework - EOL approaching, already replaced by React
Preact as alternative to React - rejected early, no new evidence
CRDT sync mechanism - explicitly rejected for this project
Obsidian native Canvas (.canvas files, node/edge system) - project uses custom whiteboard, not native Canvas
Standalone mastery display panel / 5-dim Beta-Bayesian fusion - user confirmed not rigid need, no academic precedent
Static information panels - rejected in Pencil design session
Tauri as frontend shell - not compatible with Obsidian plugin model
Web Worker approach for frontend compute - rejected in brainstorming
Browser standalone HTML demo approach - must be within Obsidian
Obsidian native UI components as primary UI - replaced by shadcn/ui
Pure Socratic auto-guidance interaction model - user rejected, replaced by user-choice
Direct answer solution for exam/whiteboard - academically proven to decrease learning effect
Self-built whiteboard from scratch - no successful precedent, explicitly rejected
HyDE, Self-RAG, KG-RAG, CRAG, LongLLMLingua - all rejected RAG approaches
Kafka / complex message queues - overkill for project scale
Qdrant / Milvus as vector databases - rejected alternatives
tiktoken as tokenizer - BLOCKER status rejection
Fixed character splitting / Late Chunking - rejected chunking strategies
Pure DSPy automation route - rejected
Full OpenCode/Agentic route - user explicitly rejected
Fixed-round / session-end / turn-by-turn scoring for whiteboard - all three rejected
Pure hardcoded templates for rendering - rejected
Infinite recursion in verification flow - rejected
Automatic back-writing - rejected
Strategy adapter intermediate layer - rejected as over-engineering
Independent orchestrator module - rejected as over-engineering
Aggressive real-time indexing - rejected
notes: CRITICAL REVERSAL: React was originally rejected (2026-03-12) in favor of Svelte, but this was REVERSED on 2026-03-17. React is now the confirmed framework.
Svelte 4 is conditionally allowed for complex UI only, but Svelte 5 is absolutely rejected.
The confirmed stack (React + ReactFlow + shadcn/ui + Vite) was established on 2026-03-17 after 6-agent research.
Frontend-first + Backend sync architecture is a confirmed core architectural decision.
Product remains within Obsidian plugin framework but uses completely custom UI via React rendered in ItemView.
Svelvet was investigated as a library option for canvas whiteboard but the self-built whiteboard approach itself was rejected.
Total of 56 distinct rejected options identified across frontend, backend, and process categories.
